item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, it just goes into negatives.

Would have been an upvote otherwise.

Might want to add somthing to explain negative distance —- how about "it has entered your mind" popup or somesuch? Or a sound file that starts having non-silence at that moment for a jump scare?? I'm not sure what the wiki allows, but it would make the upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love the countdown at the end!

Wish me luck, it's finally about to hit 10… 9…

item.title: Re: Saturnine in the Thermocline
item.content:encoded:
A solid idea, but not quite!

+ Spoilers
- An eye in the sky

Project Theia is an advanced surveillance system the Foundation is using. The frequency that this system is looking for is the same frequency given off by the remains (i.e. SCP-5186). Based off of the number of instances found, and the fact one is slowly getting closer, maybe SCP-5186 is more of a real threat than first thought…

The ambiguity is intentional, but I hope that makes more sense!

item.title: Re: Saturnine in the Thermocline
item.content:encoded:
Hmmmmm.....
Let me think then

The actual SCP is the afterlife which is the whole setting, and the "monster" is actually some surveillance device made to monitor the dead people for some nefarious Foundation reasons.

item.title: Re: Saturnine in the Thermocline
item.content:encoded:
Theia is the Greek Titan goddess of sight and prophesy, as well as being associated with the Sun and Moon. Project Theia has some very close similarities to these ideas.

As a hint, this last collapsible references 'surveillance data'.

item.title: Re: Saturnine in the Thermocline
item.content:encoded:
I searched?

What is the word "Theia"'s relevance and meaning here?

item.title: Re: Saturnine in the Thermocline
item.content:encoded:
Cheers! I can't say this came to me in one go, but reading around the site has certainly helped, plus all the feedback you guys gave! Glad you picked up on that final part too (;

Edit: Having thought about what you've said, I have changed the ending of the second exploration log (naughty of me I know). Ambiguity is important for this, and so I aim to maintain it with this change. Hopefully it works better to maintain the creepiness!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to see this finally go up :) stay the hell away if you ask me! +1

item.title: Saturnine in the Thermocline
item.content:encoded:
I’m still holding out that the loss of ambiguity here is missing out on one hell of a thematic opportunity, but your actual narrative works and your writing flows well. For a first-time author, your dialogue is pretty darn impressive. Considering modern uses for the mythical name Theia, that last collapsible adds some subtle implications that end this article on a really intriguing note.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Beware beware the depths that hunger…

Hello all! First time author here. This piece went through a lot of revisions, but ultimately I think it was worth it. Think of this as my love letter to Lovecraftian horror and good old fashioned thalassophobia! You ever went swimming in a large body of water and think 'there could literally be anything beneath me right now'?

Yeah, there really could be…

Big thanks to caspian2, cybersqyd, OCuin, Sonderance and Francis Scalia for their feedback. It was all greatly appreciated, and their advice helped shape this big time!

Image Credits:

+ Show Links
- Hide Links

Buoys in the water - https://www.pxfuel.com/en/free-photo-jxgmk
Chimera - https://pxhere.com/en/photo/606433
Sketch by yours truly!
